newsSinceJun6: No material news since June 6. Only scheduled item is a fireside chat at the Wells Fargo 16th Annual Industrials & Materials Conference today (June 9, 2:15 PM CT) - an appearance, not a catalyst. The data-center power narrative (raised FY2026 guidance to +8-11% revenue, lifted 2030 targets to $45-50B, new 4MW natural-gas data-center engine) all dates to the May Q1 print and is already in the price after a ~110% 1-yr run. Today's -2.19% is routine industrials drift/profit-taking, not fundamental.

sources.label: Cummins Investor Relations - events calendar (Wells Fargo conference Jun 9)
Cummins Q1 2026 results and raised full-year outlook (May)
Simply Wall St - CMI valuation after higher 2030 guidance and new data center power engine
Motley Fool - Cummins helping power AI data centers (May 28)

verdicts.rankRationale: Hold at 64: no new evidence since June 6 and yesterday's hold stands; the -2.2% pullback marginally improves entry asymmetry but on a crowding-sensitive alpha page a $91B, +110% 1-yr, well-publicized AI power name already sits correctly in the Monitor/benchmark bucket far from the passive/power bottleneck core.
verdicts.bull: Power Systems is the company's growth engine with record sales on data-center backup demand, raised FY26 guidance (+8-11% revenue, 17.75-18.5% EBITDA) and a new 4MW gas engine aimed squarely at data-center prime power.
verdicts.neutral: Quality industrial with genuine AI-adjacent exposure, but the rerating has happened (+26% YTD, ~110% 1-yr) and the data-center story is now consensus; today's fireside chat is unlikely to add new information.
verdicts.bear: Mega-cap, fully discovered, and only partially exposed to data centers (legacy truck engines still dominate); crowded passive ownership and a cyclical on-highway core mean limited residual alpha and downside if AI capex digestion hits genset orders.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Large prime-power/gas-engine data-center orders or further Power Systems-led guidance raises in H2 2026.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Post-rerating multiple compression if data-center genset order momentum slows or the on-highway truck cycle rolls over.
